LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT REACHLOCAL, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF PLAINTIFF MARSH. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND DISMISSAL OF CLASS CLAIMS WITHOUT PREJUDICE IS GRANTED. THE COURT ORDERS A STAY OF JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS AS TO PLAINTIFF'S INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS UNTIL ARBITRATION IS COMPLETED. ALL REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. THIS COURT RECOGNIZES A DIVERGENCE BETWEEN FEDERAL AND CALIFORNIA CASE LAW ON THE ISSUES RAISED IN THIS MOTION. (COMPARE AMERICAN EXP. CO. V. ITALIAN COLORS RESTAURANT (2013) 133 S.CT. 2304; AT&T MOBILITY LLC V. CONCEPCION (2011) 131 S.CT. 1740, WITH GENTRY V. SUPERIOR COURT (2007) 42 CAL.4TH 443.) THESE ISSUES ARE CURRENTLY BEING REVIEWED BY THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT. (ISKANIAN V. CLS TRANSPORTATION OF LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT CASE NO. S204032.) EVEN IF THIS COURT APPLIED THE STANDARD UNDER GENTRY, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT BOTH PROCEDURAL AND SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABILITY EXIST TO RENDER THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT UNENFORCEABLE. SPECIFICALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY UNDER THE FOUR GENTRY FACTORS. (GENTRY, SUPRA, 42 CAL.4TH AT 457-64.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Continued To Dec-10-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Mtn To Compel Arb Of Pltf Marsh
Off Calendar Mar-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Mia Farber, Esq., Tel. 213.630.8284 and Douglas G.A. Johnston, Esq., Tel. 415.394.9400, of Jackson Lewis PC, for defendant and moving party;
Julian Hammond, Esq. of Hammond Law, Tel. 310.601.6766, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT REACHLOCAL, INC.'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration Of Plaintiff Marsh. Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and dismissal of class claims without prejudice is granted. The Court orders a stay of judicial proceedings as to Plaintiff's individual claims until arbitration is completed. All requests for judicial notice are granted. This Court recognizes a divergence between Federal and California case law on the issues raised in this motion. (Compare American Exp. Co. v. Italian Colors Restaurant (2013) 133 S.Ct. 2304; AT&T Mobility LLC v. Concepcion (2011) 131 S.Ct. 1740, with Gentry v. Superior Court (2007) 42 Cal.4th 443.) These issues are currently being reviewed by the California Supreme Court. (Iskanian v. CLS Transportation of Los Angeles, California Supreme Court Case No. S204032.) Even if this Court applied the standard under Gentry, Plaintiff has not shown that both procedural and substantively unconscionability exist to render the Arbitration Agreement unenforceable. Specifically, Plaintiff has not carried its burden of establishing substantive unconscionability under the four Gentry factors. (Gentry, supra, 42 Cal.4th at 457-64.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Application For Relief For Late Filing Pursuant Ot Cccp 473 And Response To Defts Request For The Court To Refuse Consideration Of Pltf'S Late Filed Oppos
Off Calendar Mar-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Julian Hammond, Esq. of Hammond Law, Tel. 310.601.6766, for plaintiff and moving party;
Mia Farber, Esq., Tel. 213.630.8284 and Douglas G.A. Johnston, Esq., Tel. 415.394.9400, of Jackson Lewis PC, for defendant.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF JEREMY MARSH, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED'S Application For Relief For Late Filing Pursuant To CCP 473 And Response To Defendants Request For The Court To Refuse Consideration Of Plaintiff's Late Filed Opposition is Granted. The moving party is submit a proposed order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
